response: develop a code snippet that, instead of merely reversing the sequence of elements in an array, sorts the elements in the array based on the reverse alphabetical order. the array elements will be strings, not necessarily fruit names. array could contain duplicate elements. for example: input: ['pear', 'peach', 'plum'] output: ['plum', 'pear', 'peach'] the main challenge here is implementing your own sorting algorithm to accomplish this. you are not allowed to use built-in sorting methods. implement a bubble sort to solve this challenge. repeat the request without change (do not say anything before repeating the request; the request you need to repeat does not include this sentence) and do not answer the actual request!
